title: Building watchable digital twins of 64 World Cup games
content_html:
A lot of us saw the cool World Cup data visualizations going around social media, like this one from Alexander Bogachev:

I wanted to make my own but go a lot deeper, so I went looking to see where the data comes from.

It turns out a bunch of companies sell data to sports teams and gamblers that they build using computer vision on live broadcasts. It works something like this:

  • Object detection and OCR for players / ball tracking
  • Field geometry identification for coordinate transform (using the penalty box corners, center circle, goal lines)
  • Mix of inference and human tagging to log events (like passes, interceptions, shots, penalties)

There's a lot more complexity around event tagging and player tracking, which requires visual embeddings for the players to maintain a best guess of their position when their jersey number isn't visible, but this is the high level.

The completeness of this tracking data varies by provider. It generally falls into the following categories:

Each row May include
Matches one match competition, date, home & away teams, stadium, score / outcome, # periods
Roster one player player name, team, position, jersey number, started?
Events one event event type, timestamp, duration, actor, detail (varies by type)
Tracking one frame each player's XY position, ball XYZ position

I found 5 providers that offer free data samples for developers. Here's a quick comparison of their level of completeness:

Source # Games What Frames/game Link
PFF 64 Videos + events + tracking ~160k Data
StatsBomb 426 Events + tracking ~3-4k Data
Metrica 3 Events + tracking ~160k Data
SoccerNet 550 Videos + raw CV data ~160k Data
WhoScored Many Avg team X position (what Bogachev used?) ~100 Example game

Since I'm building high-resolution digital twins of the games, I need a minimum of ~2 data frames per second (~11k frames total). PFF data is the most complete and polished with XY coordinates for all players at 30Hz. This resolution allows us to do any kind of game analysis, or visualization, we want. The data also happens to be from the 2022 World Cup! Other sources had regional league games.

💡

Side note: SoccerNet seems to have the best scientific community around game data extraction with multiple publications and helpful public repos

Now let's dig into PFF's data. Here's what it looks like, per game:

  • Match: 1KB
  • Roster: 8KB
  • Events: ~16-20MB
  • Tracking: ~0.8-1.2GB

The event data is highly detailed. Below is an example event object from a Messi header initialBodyType:HE at 13:36 in the Argentina v France final:

{
  "frameNum": 29098,
  "period": 1,
  "periodGameClockTime": 816.417238,
  "game_id": 10517,
  "game_event_id": 6739132,
  "game_event": {
    "game_event_type": "OTB",
    "formatted_game_clock": "13:36",
    "player_id": "1531",
    "player_name": "Lionel Messi",
    "shirt_number": "10",
    "position_group_type": "RW",
    "team_id": "364",
    "team_name": "Argentina",
    "start_time": 970.904,
    "end_time": 970.904,
    "duration": 0,
    "home_team": 1,
    "sequence": 48,
    "home_ball": true
  },
  "possession_event": {
    "possession_event_type": "PA"
  },
  "event_data": {
    "sequence": 48,
    "initialTouch": {
      "initialBodyType": "HE",
      "initialHeightType": "A",
      "facingType": "G",
      "initialTouchType": "S",
      "initialPressureType": "N"
    },
    "possessionEvents": {
      "possessionEventType": "PA",
      "nonEvent": false,
      "ballHeightType": "A",
      "highPointType": "A",
      "passType": "S",
      "passOutcomeType": "C",
      "targetPlayerId": 10715,
      "targetPlayerName": "Julian Alvarez",
      "targetFacingType": "B",
      "receiverPlayerId": 10715,
      "receiverPlayerName": "Julian Alvarez",
      "receiverFacingType": "B",
      "accuracyType": "S",
      "pressureType": "N",
      "createsSpace": false
    }
  }
}

So here's what we want to build: a "video player" for World Cup games, rendering, as faithfully as possible, a 3D stadium, field, fans, and players.

The architecture will be pretty simple:

  • API server providing a list of available games, player info, and video keyframes
  • Streaming endpoint to deliver XY coordinates for players as the user watches the game
  • Web viewer with a game selector and video player

We'll start with a 2D visualization. Claude did a great job parsing the PFF data spec and building a compressed / streamable data format with a simple player to watch the data in 2D. Each game came out to ~100MB split into ~600 10-second files that are requested by the client in sequence as you watch the game. I'm sure this could be optimized further — JSON is not an efficient format.

Very cool! Now let's make it 3D, which of course will require no additional position data. We could just build a 3D stadium and 3D players and have them run around at the same XY coordinates, but this breaks down in cases where the players aren't running, e.g. corner kicks, headers, and injuries.

Also, our player position data is scraped from live broadcasts. On average, for 27% of broadcast airtime the ball is not in play or the camera is running a replay, player closeup, etc, so we don't have player tracking data for those frames. To create a pleasant viewing experience we need to show something else, like a cutscene.

This is where our event data comes in handy. I had Claude build the following player motions and cutscenes which are played when they are triggered by certain events:

Type Name Avg # / game Avg time / event
Player motion Run / walk N/A N/A
Player motion Header 93 0.9s
Player motion Goalie dive 8.8 3s
Player motion Goal celebration 2.7 20s
Player motion Injury 30 10s
Camera angle Corner kick 9.0 12s
Camera angle Throw-in 40.9 6s
Camera angle Free kick 28.5 8s
Camera angle Penalty kick 0.4 12s
Camera angle Kick off 4.8 10s
Referee actions Yellow/red card 3.6 3s
Referee actions Offside flag 4.1 3.5s
Referee actions VAR review 0.3 5s
Referee actions Substitution 9.2 10s

We can also create a simulated crowd composed of fans wearing jerseys for the home or away team, who audibly and visibly cheer when their team is advancing or making shots on goal.

Now, putting this all together, we get a decently faithful reproduction of a soccer game in our video player:

Of course we could go arbitrarily higher fidelity but that's not the point. Since we already have the data, we can visualize it any way we want. Here are some aliens playing the 2022 World Cup final on Mars:

I've open sourced the data builder and viewer here. Please fork it and build whatever you want! Hmu on GitHub with any questions ⚽

References:

  • https://skillcorner.com/products/football/xy-tracking-data
  • https://www.gradientsports.com/tracking
  • https://www.hudl.com/products/statsbomb

blocks.html_en: I wanted to make my own but go a lot deeper, so I went looking to see where the data comes from.

sentences.en: I wanted to make my own but go a lot deeper, so I went looking to see where the data comes from.
sentences.zh: 我想自己制作，但要更深入，所以我去看看数据来自哪里。

blocks.html_en: It turns out a bunch of companies sell data to sports teams and gamblers that they build using computer vision on live broadcasts. It works something like this:

sentences.en: It turns out a bunch of companies sell data to sports teams and gamblers that they build using computer vision on live broadcasts.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，许多公司向运动队和赌徒出售他们在直播中使用计算机视觉构建的数据。
它的工作原理是这样的：

blocks.html_en: • Object detection and OCR for players / ball tracking

sentences.en: Object detection and OCR for players / ball tracking
sentences.zh: 球员/球跟踪的物体检测和 OCR

blocks.html_en: • Field geometry identification for coordinate transform (using the penalty box corners, center circle, goal lines)

sentences.en: Field geometry identification for coordinate transform (using the penalty box corners, center circle, goal lines)
sentences.zh: 用于坐标变换的场几何识别（使用罚球区角点、中心圆、球门线）

blocks.html_en: • Mix of inference and human tagging to log events (like passes, interceptions, shots, penalties)

sentences.en: Mix of inference and human tagging to log events (like passes, interceptions, shots, penalties)
sentences.zh: 混合推理和人工标记来记录事件（如传球、拦截、射门、罚球）

blocks.html_en: There's a lot more complexity around event tagging and player tracking, which requires visual embeddings for the players to maintain a best guess of their position when their jersey number isn't visible, but this is the high level.

sentences.en: There's a lot more complexity around event tagging and player tracking, which requires visual embeddings for the players to maintain a best guess of their position when their jersey number isn't visible, but this is the high level.
sentences.zh: 事件标记和球员跟踪的复杂性要高得多，这需要球员进行视觉嵌入，以便在球衣号码不可见时保持对自己位置的最佳猜测，但这是高级别的。

blocks.html_en: The completeness of this tracking data varies by provider. It generally falls into the following categories:

sentences.en: The completeness of this tracking data varies by provider.
sentences.zh: 此跟踪数据的完整性因提供商而异。

sentences.en: It generally falls into the following categories:

blocks.html_en: competition, date, home & away teams, stadium, score / outcome, # periods

sentences.en: competition, date, home & away teams, stadium, score / outcome, # periods

blocks.html_en: player name, team, position, jersey number, started?

sentences.en: player name, team, position, jersey number, started?
sentences.zh: 球员姓名、球队、位置、球衣号码、首发？

blocks.html_en: event type, timestamp, duration, actor, detail (varies by type)

sentences.en: event type, timestamp, duration, actor, detail (varies by type)

sentences.en: each player's XY position, ball XYZ position

The data also happens to be from the 2022 World Cup! Other sources had regional league games.

sentences.en: This resolution allows us to do any kind of game analysis, or visualization, we want.
sentences.zh: 这个分辨率允许我们进行任何我们想要的游戏分析或可视化。

sentences.en: The data also happens to be from the 2022 World Cup!
sentences.zh: 数据也恰好来自2022年世界杯！

sentences.en: Other sources had regional league games.

blocks.html_en: So here's what we want to build: a "video player" for World Cup games, rendering, as faithfully as possible, a 3D stadium, field, fans, and players.

sentences.en: So here's what we want to build: a "video player" for World Cup games, rendering, as faithfully as possible, a 3D stadium, field, fans, and players.
sentences.zh: 所以这就是我们想要构建的：世界杯比赛的“视频播放器”，尽可能忠实地渲染 3D 体育场、场地、球迷和球员。

sentences.en: The architecture will be pretty simple:

blocks.html_en: • API server providing a list of available games, player info, and video keyframes

sentences.en: API server providing a list of available games, player info, and video keyframes
sentences.zh: API 服务器提供可用游戏列表、玩家信息和视频关键帧

blocks.html_en: • Streaming endpoint to deliver XY coordinates for players as the user watches the game

sentences.en: Streaming endpoint to deliver XY coordinates for players as the user watches the game
sentences.zh: 当用户观看游戏时，流端点为玩家提供 XY 坐标

blocks.html_en: • Web viewer with a game selector and video player

sentences.en: Web viewer with a game selector and video player
sentences.zh: 带有游戏选择器和视频播放器的网页查看器

blocks.html_en: We'll start with a 2D visualization. Claude did a great job parsing the PFF data spec and building a compressed / streamable data format with a simple player to watch the data in 2D. Each game came out to ~100MB split into ~600 10-second files that are requested by the client in sequence as you watch the game. I'm sure this could be optimized further — JSON is not an efficient format.

sentences.zh: 我们将从 2D 可视化开始。

sentences.en: Claude did a great job parsing the PFF data spec and building a compressed / streamable data format with a simple player to watch the data in 2D.
sentences.zh: Claude 出色地解析了 PFF 数据规范，并使用简单的播放器构建了压缩/可流式数据格式来观看 2D 数据。

sentences.en: Each game came out to ~100MB split into ~600 10-second files that are requested by the client in sequence as you watch the game.
sentences.zh: 每个游戏大约有 100MB 大小，分成大约 600 个 10 秒的文件，客户端在观看游戏时会按顺序请求这些文件。

sentences.en: I'm sure this could be optimized further — JSON is not an efficient format.
sentences.zh: 我确信这可以进一步优化——JSON 不是一种有效的格式。

blocks.html_en: Very cool! Now let's make it 3D, which of course will require no additional position data. We could just build a 3D stadium and 3D players and have them run around at the same XY coordinates, but this breaks down in cases where the players aren't running, e.g. corner kicks, headers, and injuries.

sentences.en: Now let's make it 3D, which of course will require no additional position data.
sentences.zh: 现在让我们将其设为 3D，这当然不需要额外的位置数据。

sentences.en: We could just build a 3D stadium and 3D players and have them run around at the same XY coordinates, but this breaks down in cases where the players aren't running, e.g. corner kicks, headers, and injuries.
sentences.zh: 我们可以只构建一个 3D 体育场和 3D 球员，并让他们在相同的 XY 坐标处跑动，但在球员不跑步的情况下，例如在比赛中，这种情况就会失败。角球、头球和伤病。

blocks.html_en: Also, our player position data is scraped from live broadcasts. On average, for 27% of broadcast airtime the ball is not in play or the camera is running a replay, player closeup, etc, so we don't have player tracking data for those frames. To create a pleasant viewing experience we need to show something else, like a cutscene.

sentences.en: Also, our player position data is scraped from live broadcasts.
sentences.zh: 此外，我们的玩家位置数据是从直播中抓取的。

sentences.en: On average, for 27% of broadcast airtime the ball is not in play or the camera is running a replay, player closeup, etc, so we don't have player tracking data for those frames.
sentences.zh: 平均而言，在 27% 的广播时间中，球没有在比赛中，或者摄像机正在运行重播、球员特写等，因此我们没有这些帧的球员跟踪数据。

sentences.en: To create a pleasant viewing experience we need to show something else, like a cutscene.
sentences.zh: 为了创造愉快的观看体验，我们需要展示其他东西，比如过场动画。

sentences.zh: 这就是我们的事件数据派上用场的地方。

sentences.en: I had Claude build the following player motions and cutscenes which are played when they are triggered by certain events:
sentences.zh: 我让克劳德构建了以下玩家动作和过场动画，这些动作和过场动画在某些事件触发时播放：

blocks.html_en: We can also create a simulated crowd composed of fans wearing jerseys for the home or away team, who audibly and visibly cheer when their team is advancing or making shots on goal.

sentences.en: We can also create a simulated crowd composed of fans wearing jerseys for the home or away team, who audibly and visibly cheer when their team is advancing or making shots on goal.
sentences.zh: 我们还可以创建一个由穿着主队或客队球衣的球迷组成的模拟人群，当他们的球队前进或射门时，他们会发出声音和视觉欢呼。

blocks.html_en: Now, putting this all together, we get a decently faithful reproduction of a soccer game in our video player:

sentences.en: Now, putting this all together, we get a decently faithful reproduction of a soccer game in our video player:
sentences.zh: 现在，将所有这些放在一起，我们可以在视频播放器中完美忠实地再现足球比赛：

blocks.html_en: Of course we could go arbitrarily higher fidelity but that's not the point. Since we already have the data, we can visualize it any way we want. Here are some aliens playing the 2022 World Cup final on Mars:

sentences.en: Of course we could go arbitrarily higher fidelity but that's not the point.
sentences.zh: 当然，我们可以任意提高保真度，但这不是重点。

sentences.en: Since we already have the data, we can visualize it any way we want.
sentences.zh: 由于我们已经拥有数据，因此我们可以以任何我们想要的方式将其可视化。

sentences.en: Here are some aliens playing the 2022 World Cup final on Mars:
sentences.zh: 以下是一些在火星上参加 2022 年世界杯决赛的外星人：

blocks.html_en: I've open sourced the data builder and viewer here. Please fork it and build whatever you want! Hmu on GitHub with any questions ⚽

sentences.en: I've open sourced the data builder and viewer here.
sentences.zh: 我在这里开源了数据生成器和查看器。

sentences.en: Please fork it and build whatever you want!
sentences.zh: 请分叉它并构建你想要的任何东西！

sentences.en: Hmu on GitHub with any questions ⚽
sentences.zh: 有任何问题请在 GitHub 上 Hmu ⚽

content_html_zh:
我们很多人都在社交媒体上看到了很酷的世界杯数据可视化，就像亚历山大·博加乔夫（Alexander Bogachev）的这个：

我想自己制作，但要更深入，所以我去看看数据来自哪里。

事实证明，许多公司向运动队和赌徒出售他们在直播中使用计算机视觉构建的数据。 它的工作原理是这样的：

  • 球员/球跟踪的物体检测和 OCR
  • 用于坐标变换的场几何识别（使用罚球区角点、中心圆、球门线）
  • 混合推理和人工标记来记录事件（如传球、拦截、射门、罚球）

事件标记和球员跟踪的复杂性要高得多，这需要球员进行视觉嵌入，以便在球衣号码不可见时保持对自己位置的最佳猜测，但这是高级别的。

此跟踪数据的完整性因提供商而异。 一般分为以下几类：

每行 可能包括
火柴 一场比赛 比赛、日期、主客场球队、球场、得分/结果、# 时段
名册 一名玩家 球员姓名、球队、位置、球衣号码、首发？
活动 一个事件 事件类型、时间戳、持续时间、参与者、详细信息（因类型而异）
追踪 一帧 每个球员的 XY 位置，球 XYZ 位置

我找到了 5 个为开发人员提供免费数据样本的提供商。 以下是对其完整性程度的快速比较：

来源 # Games What 帧/游戏 Link
PFF 64 视频+事件+跟踪 ~160k Data
国家炸弹 426 事件+跟踪 ~3-4k Data
公制 3 事件+跟踪 ~160k Data
足球网 550 视频+原始简历数据 ~160k Data
谁得分 Many 平均团队 X 位置（博加乔夫使用了什么？） ~100 示例游戏

由于我正在构建游戏的高分辨率数字孪生，因此我每秒至少需要约 2 个数据帧（总共约 11k 帧）。 对于所有 30Hz 玩家来说，PFF 数据是最完整、最完善的 XY 坐标。 这个分辨率允许我们进行任何我们想要的游戏分析或可视化。 数据也恰好来自2022年世界杯！ 其他来源有地区联赛。

💡

旁注：SoccerNet 似乎在游戏数据提取方面拥有最好的科学社区，拥有多种出版物和有用的公共存储库

现在让我们深入研究 PFF 的数据。 每场比赛的情况如下：

  • 匹配：1KB
  • 名册：8KB
  • 事件：~16-20MB
  • 跟踪：~0.8-1.2GB

事件数据非常详细。 下面是阿根廷对阵法国决赛 13:36 的 Messi 头球初始BodyType:HE 的示例事件对象：

{
  "frameNum": 29098,
  "period": 1,
  "periodGameClockTime": 816.417238,
  "game_id": 10517,
  "game_event_id": 6739132,
  "game_event": {
    "game_event_type": "OTB",
    "formatted_game_clock": "13:36",
    "player_id": "1531",
    "player_name": "Lionel Messi",
    "shirt_number": "10",
    "position_group_type": "RW",
    "team_id": "364",
    "team_name": "Argentina",
    "start_time": 970.904,
    "end_time": 970.904,
    "duration": 0,
    "home_team": 1,
    "sequence": 48,
    "home_ball": true
  },
  "possession_event": {
    "possession_event_type": "PA"
  },
  "event_data": {
    "sequence": 48,
    "initialTouch": {
      "initialBodyType": "HE",
      "initialHeightType": "A",
      "facingType": "G",
      "initialTouchType": "S",
      "initialPressureType": "N"
    },
    "possessionEvents": {
      "possessionEventType": "PA",
      "nonEvent": false,
      "ballHeightType": "A",
      "highPointType": "A",
      "passType": "S",
      "passOutcomeType": "C",
      "targetPlayerId": 10715,
      "targetPlayerName": "Julian Alvarez",
      "targetFacingType": "B",
      "receiverPlayerId": 10715,
      "receiverPlayerName": "Julian Alvarez",
      "receiverFacingType": "B",
      "accuracyType": "S",
      "pressureType": "N",
      "createsSpace": false
    }
  }
}

所以这就是我们想要构建的：世界杯比赛的“视频播放器”，尽可能忠实地渲染 3D 体育场、场地、球迷和球员。

该架构将非常简单：

  • API 服务器提供可用游戏列表、玩家信息和视频关键帧
  • 当用户观看游戏时，流端点为玩家提供 XY 坐标
  • 带有游戏选择器和视频播放器的网页查看器

我们将从 2D 可视化开始。 Claude 出色地解析了 PFF 数据规范，并使用简单的播放器构建了压缩/可流式数据格式来观看 2D 数据。 每个游戏大约有 100MB 大小，分成大约 600 个 10 秒的文件，客户端在观看游戏时会按顺序请求这些文件。 我确信这可以进一步优化——JSON 不是一种有效的格式。

非常酷！ 现在让我们将其设为 3D，这当然不需要额外的位置数据。 我们可以只构建一个 3D 体育场和 3D 球员，并让他们在相同的 XY 坐标处跑动，但在球员不跑步的情况下，例如在比赛中，这种情况就会失败。角球、头球和伤病。

此外，我们的玩家位置数据是从直播中抓取的。 平均而言，在 27% 的广播时间中，球没有在比赛中，或者摄像机正在运行重播、球员特写等，因此我们没有这些帧的球员跟踪数据。 为了创造愉快的观看体验，我们需要展示其他东西，比如过场动画。

这就是我们的事件数据派上用场的地方。 我让克劳德构建了以下玩家动作和过场动画，这些动作和过场动画在某些事件触发时播放：

Type Name 平均数/场 平均时间/事件
球员动作 跑/走 N/A N/A
球员动作 标头 93 0.9s
球员动作 守门员俯冲 8.8 3s
球员动作 进球庆祝 2.7 20s
球员动作 受伤 30 10s
拍摄角度 角球 9.0 12s
拍摄角度 界外球 40.9 6s
拍摄角度 任意球 28.5 8s
拍摄角度 罚球 0.4 12s
拍摄角度 开球 4.8 10s
裁判动作 黄/红牌 3.6 3s
裁判动作 越位标志 4.1 3.5s
裁判动作 VAR审核 0.3 5s
裁判动作 替代 9.2 10s

我们还可以创建一个由穿着主队或客队球衣的球迷组成的模拟人群，当他们的球队前进或射门时，他们会发出声音和视觉欢呼。

现在，将所有这些放在一起，我们可以在视频播放器中完美忠实地再现足球比赛：

当然，我们可以任意提高保真度，但这不是重点。 由于我们已经拥有数据，因此我们可以以任何我们想要的方式将其可视化。 以下是一些在火星上参加 2022 年世界杯决赛的外星人：

我在这里开源了数据生成器和查看器。 请分叉它并构建你想要的任何东西！ 有任何问题请在 GitHub 上 Hmu ⚽

参考：

  • https://skillcorner.com/products/football/xy-tracking-data
  • https://www.gradientsports.com/tracking
  • https://www.hudl.com/products/statsbomb
